TWEET 1
OpenAI just partnered with Accenture, BCG, McKinsey, and Capgemini to push its Frontier agentic AI platform into the enterprise. This is not a product launch. It is a distribution strategy — and it changes the competitive landscape.

---

TWEET 2
These four consulting firms touch the majority of Fortune 500 transformation budgets. When they recommend an agentic platform, that recommendation carries procurement weight far beyond any technical benchmark.

---

TWEET 3
The playbook is familiar: it mirrors how cloud adoption was driven through consulting channels a decade ago. But the timeline is compressed. Agentic AI decisions made in the next two quarters will lock in operational architecture for years.

---

TWEET 4
The risk for enterprises: when the consultancy managing your transformation is incentivized to deploy one vendor's agent platform, switching costs compound before you realize the dependency exists.

## Fact-check notes
- OpenAI announced 'Frontier Alliances' partnerships with Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey on February 23, 2026 (Source: SiliconAngle)
- The partnerships are designed to help enterprise customers adopt OpenAI's Frontier platform for building, deploying, and orchestrating AI-powered agents (Source: SiliconAngle)
- Frontier is OpenAI's recently launched agentic AI platform for building, deploying, and orchestrating AI-powered co-workers (Source: SiliconAngle)
